steps.title: Build the length model
steps.content: The chain already has length $33$. Each added segment has length $8-r$, and $x$ segments are added. So the total length is $$f(x)=33+x(8-r).$$

steps.title: Use a quick table check
steps.content: To check the structure quickly, choose an easy value such as $r=3$. Then each new segment is $8-3=5$ cm, so the lengths for $x=0,1,2$ are $33,38,43$. A two-point line calculation gives $f(x)=5x+33$ for this test case.

steps.title: Match the symbolic form
steps.content: Distribute the general expression: $$33+x(8-r)=33+8x-rx=8x-rx+33.$$ That matches the keyed choice B.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is the keyed distributed form of the model: $8x-rx+33$. Choice A leaves the added-segment part factored as $33+(8-r)x$, so it is not the keyed distributed form. Choice C fails to multiply $r$ by $x$, and choice D incorrectly multiplies the starting length by $x$. Therefore, B is correct.
